commit.message:
feat(web-ui): add Create Agent Profile dialog to pool detail

Add CreateProfileDialog component with 8 optional policy/template ID
fields, wired to the existing useCreateAgentProfile mutation hook.
Place an 'Add Agent Profile' button in the PoolDetailPage's agent
profiles section header. Include 12 component tests covering rendering,
submission, error handling, form reset, and endpoint validation.

Completes T128 (E025: Web UI Creation & Editing Forms).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(web-ui): add Reassign Pool operator action to task detail

Wire the existing useReassignPool hook and POST /tasks/:id/actions/reassign-pool
API endpoint into the TaskActionBar UI component.

Changes:
- Add reassign-pool to action-definitions.ts (type, defs, STATUS_ACTIONS)
- Create ReassignPoolDialog with pool selector dropdown and reason textarea
- Wire useReassignPool mutation in TaskActionBar with dialog lifecycle
- Add to BACKLOG, READY, BLOCKED, ASSIGNED, IN_DEVELOPMENT, ESCALATED statuses
- Update existing action-definitions tests for new status mappings
- Add integration tests for full dialog flow and terminal state exclusion

Closes T131

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(web-ui): add Edit Task dialog to task detail page

Add EditTaskDialog component that allows operators to edit task metadata
from the task detail page. The dialog pre-populates all editable fields
from the current task data and uses optimistic concurrency control via
the version field.

Features:
- Edit button with pencil icon in task detail header
- Pre-populated form for all mutable fields (title, description,
  priority, risk level, estimated size, external ref, severity,
  acceptance criteria, definition of done, required capabilities,
  suggested file scope)
- Only changed fields sent in update payload
- 409 Conflict handling with user-friendly message
- 15 component tests + 2 integration tests in TaskDetailPage

Closes T129

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): clean orphaned worktrees on startup (T149)

Add WorkspaceCleanupService that runs during application bootstrap to
scan workspace directories, cross-reference with active leases in the
database, and auto-clean orphaned worktrees exceeding the retention
period (default 7 days, configurable via WORKSPACE_RETENTION_DAYS).

- Scans {WORKSPACES_ROOT}/{repoId}/{taskId}/ directory structure
- Never deletes worktrees with non-terminal leases (safety invariant)
- Orphans older than retention → deleted with size estimation
- Recent orphans → logged as pending cleanup
- Error isolation: one directory failure doesn't prevent others
- Cleans empty repo directories after workspace deletion
- 17 unit tests with fake filesystem for deterministic testing
- Registered WorkspaceCleanupModule in AppModule after StartupDiagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(m9): namespace CRUD, dynamic CRD CRUD, network resources, config resources

Complete M9 Wave 2 — remaining resource type implementations:

Namespace Management (#110):
- Engine: create_namespace/delete_namespace in namespace.rs
- Desktop + Hub: commands and routes for namespace CRUD
- Frontend: list page with create/delete, protected namespace guardrails

Dynamic CRD Instance CRUD (#119):
- Engine: new dynamic.rs module using kube-rs DynamicObject
- Supports list/get/apply/delete for arbitrary custom resources
- CRD instance page: create button, per-row delete
- Detail page: loads unknown kinds via dynamic API, enables YAML edit

Additional Wave 2 fixes:
- NetworkPolicies page fixes
- Clippy lint fix for dynamic.rs too_many_arguments

Validated: cargo fmt ✅, clippy ✅, 111 Rust tests ✅,
hub + desktop compile ✅, web build ✅, 36 web tests ✅
All 16 M9 issues verified and closed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add node operations — cordon, uncordon, drain, taint management (#117)

Implement node operations across the full stack:

- New engine module (crates/engine/src/node_ops.rs): cordon, uncordon,
  drain (with DaemonSet/mirror pod filtering and Eviction API), add/remove taints
- Desktop commands: cordon_node, uncordon_node, drain_node, add_node_taint,
  remove_node_taint with audit logging
- Hub routes: POST nodes/:name/cordon, uncordon, drain, taints;
  DELETE nodes/:name/taints/:key
- API wrappers: cordonNode, uncordonNode, drainNode, addNodeTaint, removeNodeTaint
- UI: scheduling status badge, cordon/uncordon toggle, drain dialog with
  grace period/force/ignore-daemonsets options, taint management panel

Closes #117

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(web-ui): add Import Tasks multi-step dialog

Build a 4-step wizard dialog for importing tasks from local directories:
- Step 1: Path input with optional glob pattern and scan button
- Step 2: Preview table with checkboxes, parse warnings, editable names

feat(web-ui): add Create Project dialog to dashboard

Add CreateProjectDialog component with name (required), owner (required),
and description (optional) fields. Wire it to the existing useCreateProject
hook. Place the 'Create Project' button on the Dashboard page for initial
setup flow access.

- Client-side validation enforces non-empty name and owner

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): add startup recovery diagnostics logging (T148)

Add StartupDiagnosticsService that runs on application bootstrap to
detect and log pending recovery items from unclean shutdowns:

- Stale leases: heartbeat > 75s old (matching reconciliation sweep policy)
- Orphaned jobs: CLAIMED/RUNNING status > 10 min old
- Stuck tasks: ASSIGNED status > 5 min old

Clean startup logs reassuring INFO message; recovery items log WARN
with counts. Errors are caught so diagnostics never block startup.

15 unit tests cover all detection scenarios and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add ops routes: exec, port-forward, list-containers, log-stream
- Add Helm routes: history, values (with redaction), rollback
- Add misc routes: active-context, namespace, resource-counts,

- Update api.ts web fallbacks from stubs to real HTTP calls

P2 Frontend Fixes:

  namespace, auto-refresh interval, production patterns)
- Add global +error.svelte error boundary
- Fix breadcrumb, SearchPalette, pod owner link, CRD navigation

P3 Documentation:
- Add LICENSE (MIT), CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md
- Add docs/DEPLOYMENT.md and docs/API_REFERENCE.md

Validated: cargo fmt ✅, clippy ✅, 107 Rust tests ✅,
hub + desktop compile ✅, web lint ✅, build ✅, 36 web tests ✅

commit.message:
feat(cli): make factory init idempotent (T144)

- Read .copilot-factory.json marker at init start to detect re-runs
- Update existing project/repository metadata instead of duplicating
- Fall back to ON CONFLICT name-based lookup when marker is missing
- Extract insertProject() and insertRepository() helper functions
- Add 5 new tests covering metadata updates, marker-less re-init,
  repository updates, and task dedup on re-import
- All 4,390 tests pass, lint clean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(worker-runner): re-export dispatch and supervisor types from @factory/application

- Add @factory/application as dependency of @factory/worker-runner
- Re-export WorkerDispatchService, WorkerSupervisorService, and all
  related types/ports from the worker-runner package entry point
- Add unit tests verifying value exports are accessible
- Update tsconfig references for the new dependency
- Fix stale backlog index statuses (E009 now 13/13 complete)

Closes T139.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): implement POST /import/execute endpoint

Add the import execution endpoint that persists discovered tasks to the
database with automatic project/repository scaffolding and dependency
wiring, all in a single atomic transaction.

- POST /import/execute accepts tasks, projectName, and optional repo info
- Find-or-create project by name, repository by name within project
- Dedup via externalRef — skips tasks already imported to the repository
- TaskDependency records wired from externalRef cross-references
- Best-effort dependency resolution (warns on missing refs, doesn't fail)
- Tasks created in BACKLOG status with source 'automated'
- ExecuteRequestDto uses Zod 4 (control-plane version) for type compat
- 10 integration tests with in-memory SQLite covering all acceptance criteria
- Updated controller tests with execute delegation coverage

Closes T116

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): implement factory init interactive command

Add the `factory init` subcommand that registers a project with the
factory through an interactive flow:

- Auto-detects project metadata (name, git remote, branch, owner)
- Prompts for missing values via readline
- Creates Project and Repository records in SQLite
- Optionally discovers and imports tasks from a local directory
- Writes .copilot-factory.json marker file to project root
- Handles Ctrl+C gracefully without partial writes

Restructures cli.ts to support Commander subcommands while preserving
the default server-start behavior.

Closes T143.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix three existing bugs in bioc-run (#23)

* Fix three existing bugs in bioc-run

1. Fix path case inconsistency: Use uppercase R in all volume mount paths
   - Changed /usr/local/lib/r/host-site-library to /usr/local/lib/R/host-site-library
   - Affects reset ownership (-r) and permission-fixing operations
   - Critical on case-sensitive filesystems (Linux)

2. Fix variable name typo: Use HLIBUSER instead of undefined HLIBENV
   - Line 142 was checking wrong variable name
   - Caused R_LIBS_USER to always be appended to .Renviron

3. Fix regex pattern: Remove pipe from character class
   - Changed GITHUB_[P|T].* to GITHUB_[PT].*
   - Inside brackets, pipe is literal, not alternation

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Marcel Ramos Pérez <LiNk-NY@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Streaming render migration and buffer elimination

- Convert all render functions to write directly to out: TextIO
  instead of returning strings; use sep='' forward-pass pattern
  to handle separators without lookahead or wrappers.

- Eliminate lines lists in diagram body builders and tree renderers:
  _render_markdown_node, _render_html_node, _write_ltac_node_normalized,
  render_info all write directly to out using a first=[True] mutable flag.
  _sacm_collect_edges and _gsn_collect_edges now take a write_edge
  callback instead of an edge_lines list. _sacm_diagram_body uses two
  DFS passes (collect dot decls, then write edges); _gsn_diagram_body
  writes BottomPadding after edges (cosmetic ordering change in Mermaid
  source, golden files updated).

- Replace io.StringIO whole-document buffer in _inline_rewrite_file with
  direct streaming to a temp file. Line-ending detection reads only the
  first 4 KB. No content comparison after writing (mtime may change).

- Replace two-pass --fixmissing algorithm with a single streaming pass:
  _collect_document_element_ids pre-scans existing element IDs; smart
  placement (_emit_stubs_after, _emit_all_remaining) inserts missing
  stubs inline relative to their LTAC predecessors without buffering.
  Removes _scan_region_ends, _find_epilogue_line, _insert_missing_stubs.

- Add docs/streaming.md documenting the architecture, separator pattern,
  and all changed function signatures.

- Update AGENTS.md with streaming API conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): implement two-phase Ctrl+C shutdown

Add graceful drain on first SIGINT/SIGTERM that polls SQLite for active
worker leases (up to 30s), then flushes tracing and closes the app.
Second SIGINT force-kills tracked child processes and exits immediately.

- Create apps/cli/src/shutdown.ts with countActiveLeases, drain,
  forceKillChildren, setupShutdownHandlers, and childPids export
- Replace inline signal handlers in cli.ts with setupShutdownHandlers()
- Add 22 unit tests covering all exported functions
- Mark T147 as done in backlog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): complete factory start command with verbose flag and banner

Add --verbose flag for debug-level startup logging, box-drawing character
banner with project count display, and queryProjectCount() helper that
safely queries the SQLite database via read-only connection.

- Add verbose field to CliOptions and Commander config
- Rewrite printBanner() with Unicode box-drawing chars and dynamic padding
- Add queryProjectCount(dbPath) with defensive error handling
- Add 3 tests for queryProjectCount (missing DB, missing table, populated)
- Update existing tests for verbose option
- Mark T145 as done in backlog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): implement project metadata auto-detection for factory init

Add detect.ts module with five detection functions used by `factory init`
to minimize interactive prompts:

- detectProjectName: reads package.json name, falls back to dir basename
- detectGitRemoteUrl: runs `git remote get-url origin`
- detectDefaultBranch: parses git symbolic-ref, defaults to "main"
- detectOwner: reads git config user.name, falls back to OS username
- detectAll: aggregates all detections into ProjectMetadata

All functions return null on failure (never throw). 21 tests using real
temp directories and git repos validate all detection paths and fallbacks.

Closes T142.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): add POST /import/discover endpoint

Create NestJS ImportModule with discovery endpoint that accepts a local
directory path, auto-detects format (backlog.json vs markdown), runs the
appropriate parser, and returns a preview of discovered tasks without
writing to the database.

- ImportController with POST /import/discover and Swagger docs
- ImportService with format auto-detection and suggested name derivation
- Zod-validated DiscoverRequestDto (path required, pattern optional)
- Controller tests (4) and service tests (7) with FakeFileSystem
- Added JSON parser exports to @factory/infrastructure barrel

Closes T115.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

T146 (serve web-ui static files from same server) was already fully
implemented by T120's StaticServeModule in apps/control-plane/src/static-serve/.
All 7 acceptance criteria verified met.

Also fixes T120 status in backlog index (task file was done but index
said pending). Cleans up Ready-Now and P0 Pending lists to reflect
current state, adding newly-unblocked tasks T142, T145, T115.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix stray vertices causing 912m bounding box and Vision Pro crash

Root cause: Array, Solidify, and Bevel modifiers on Blender objects were not
applied before export. The USD exporter evaluated these modifiers during export,
creating geometry spanning up to 912 meters (vs actual model ~21m). This caused
GPU depth buffer issues on real Vision Pro hardware, triggering device reset.

Fix: Apply all modifiers before per-material join in export script.
Re-exported all 18 USDZ parts with modifiers applied.
Collection 7 re-exported unmerged (205 meshes, 5.8MB vs 702MB merged).

Verified: max coordinate in all parts now ≤ 21 (was 910, 430, 203, 117).
Estimated draw calls: ~315 (down from 364).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(infrastructure): add JSON/backlog.json task parser

Implement deterministic JSON task parser for the import pipeline (T114).
Supports two formats:
- backlog.json: structured format with epics/tasks arrays
- flat tasks.json: plain array of ImportedTask objects

Auto-detects format, maps all backlog.json fields to ImportedTask schema,
validates via Zod safeParse, and collects warnings for invalid entries.
Reuses mapTaskType/mapPriority from markdown parser.

30 tests covering all public functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: incluir migraciones en control de versiones

Se eliminan las reglas del .gitignore que ignoraban las carpetas 
de migraciones (apps/*/migrations/) para permitir reproducir 
el esquema en otros entornos

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): build CLI entry point command (T121)

Extract createApp() from main.ts into bootstrap.ts for reuse by the CLI.
Implement full CLI entry point with:
- Commander arg parsing (--port, --db-path, --no-open, --no-ui)
- Database migrations, tracing init, NestJS app creation
- Static web-UI serving with graceful fallback
- Browser auto-open and SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown
- 15 unit tests for arg parsing and path resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): implement programmatic Drizzle migration runner

Add runMigrations() utility to apps/cli that runs Drizzle ORM migrations
programmatically against the factory SQLite database. This eliminates the
need for manual pnpm db:migrate — migrations run automatically as part of
factory init and factory start.

Key features:
- Creates DB file and parent directories on first run
- Idempotent: no-op when already up to date
- Reports applied migration count via MigrationResult
- Custom MigrationError with dbPath/migrationsFolder context
- SQLite configured with WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys (matching control-plane)
- Always closes DB connection in finally block

Includes 10 unit tests covering first-run, idempotency, error cases,
WAL mode verification, and table creation validation.

Closes T141.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): serve web-ui static files via @fastify/static (T120)

Add StaticServeModule and configureStaticServing() function for optional
single-origin deployment where API and web-ui SPA are served from the
same Fastify instance.

Implementation:
- @fastify/static registered with wildcard:false for reply.sendFile()
- Custom GET /* route serves actual files or falls back to index.html
- Fastify route priority ensures NestJS API routes always win
- Module activated by SERVE_STATIC=true + WEB_UI_DIST env vars
- configureStaticServing() exported for CLI entry point (T121)

Tests: 12 tests covering validation, static serving, SPA fallback,
API route precedence, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial plan (#16)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): establish global data directory convention (T140)

Add paths module centralizing all path resolution for the factory's
global data directory (~/.copilot-factory/). Exports getFactoryHome(),
getDbPath(), getWorkspacesRoot(), getArtifactsRoot(), getMigrationsDir(),
and ensureFactoryHome() with FACTORY_HOME env var override support.

Includes 10 unit tests, vitest config, and test script for the CLI
workspace.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(infrastructure): implement deterministic markdown task parser

Add markdown task parser for the import pipeline (T113). Parses
directory of markdown task files into ImportManifest using existing
import schemas from T112.

Key features:
- discoverMarkdownTasks() main entry with FileSystem DI
- Metadata table parsing (| Field | Value | format)
- Section extraction (Description, Goal, Scope, etc.)
- Checkbox acceptance criteria extraction
- Dependency ref extraction from markdown links
- ExternalRef extraction from filenames (e.g., T045)
- Index.md parsing for task ordering hints
- Lenient parsing with ParseWarning collection
- Zod validation via ImportedTaskSchema

68 comprehensive tests covering all public functions and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: stamp release tag into build artifacts and filenames

- Extract version from git tag (GITHUB_REF_NAME) at build time
- Stamp version into all Cargo.toml crates and tauri.conf.json before build

- Tauri bundle installers (MSI/NSIS/DMG) inherit the stamped version automatically
- Set GitHub Release title to 'Telescope <tag>'

commit.message:
test(control-plane): add end-to-end dispatch integration test (T138)

Proves full task lifecycle BACKLOG → READY → ASSIGNED → dispatch →
IN_DEVELOPMENT → DEV_COMPLETE through the automation/dispatch pipeline
using FakeRunnerAdapter and FakeWorkspaceManager.

Verifies dispatch job completion, worker creation, heartbeat forwarding
(lease STARTING → RUNNING → COMPLETING), and task state transitions.

commit.message:
feat(control-plane): wire WorkerDispatchService into AutomationService

Wire the full worker dispatch chain into AutomationService so each
automation cycle attempts to process pending WORKER_DISPATCH jobs.

- Add createWorkerSupervisorUnitOfWork and createHeartbeatUnitOfWork
  factory functions in application-adapters.ts
- Instantiate HeartbeatService, HeartbeatForwarderAdapter,
  InfrastructureAdapters, WorkerSupervisorService, and
  WorkerDispatchService in the AutomationService constructor
- Add processWorkerDispatches() fire-and-forget method to runCycle()
- Track active dispatch promises to prevent unbounded concurrency
- Add 10 new tests covering UoW factories and dispatch integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(infrastructure): wire workspace, runtime, and packet adapters into control-plane

Create adapter bridges in apps/control-plane/src/automation/ that wrap
WorkspaceManager, WorkspacePacketMounter, and CopilotCliAdapter from
@factory/infrastructure into WorkspaceProviderPort, PacketMounterPort,
and RuntimeAdapterPort application-layer port interfaces.

- createWorkspaceProviderAdapter: maps positional params to options objects
- createPacketMounterAdapter: delegates and discards MountPacketsResult
- createRuntimeAdapterBridge: bridges SupervisorRunContext/RunContext types
- createInfrastructureAdapters: top-level factory with env config support
- resolveInfrastructureConfig: reads WORKSPACES_ROOT with defaults
- 17 unit tests covering all adapter bridges and config resolution

Closes T136.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): implement HeartbeatForwarderPort adapter (T135)

Add heartbeat-forwarder-adapter in apps/control-plane/src/automation/
that bridges WorkerSupervisor heartbeat events to the HeartbeatService.

- Factory function createHeartbeatForwarderAdapter() implementing HeartbeatForwarderPort
- Maps forwardHeartbeat(leaseId, workerId, isTerminal) to receiveHeartbeat()
- Errors caught and logged (never propagated to worker process)
- 7 unit tests covering forwarding, error handling, and resilience
- Update backlog: T132/T135 done, E009 progress 7/13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): wire WorkerDispatch unit-of-work adapter

Add createWorkerDispatchUnitOfWork() factory to application-adapters.ts,
bridging infrastructure database repositories to the WorkerDispatchUnitOfWork
port from @factory/application. Enables WorkerDispatchService to resolve
spawn context (task packet, timeout settings, workspace paths, policy
snapshot) for tasks prior to worker creation.

- Read-only adapter pattern (no write transaction needed)
- resolveSpawnContext returns null for missing/terminal tasks
- Task type to packet type mapping for all task types
- 17 unit tests covering all paths and edge cases
- Closes T134

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Codify push and release tagging policy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement WorkerDispatchService

Add WorkerDispatchService that claims WORKER_DISPATCH jobs from the queue,
resolves spawn context via WorkerDispatchContextPort, and delegates to
WorkerSupervisorService.spawnWorker(). Follows the existing job-processing
pattern (claim → process → complete/fail) but is async and non-self-
rescheduling since dispatch jobs are created on-demand by the scheduler.

gap between task scheduling and worker execution. The scheduler creates

in ASSIGNED state.

- T132: WorkerDispatchService in application layer

- T136: Wire workspace/runtime/packet infrastructure adapters

commit.message:
fix: add @Inject() to all controllers/services for tsx compatibility

Extends the previous EventsModule fix to all controllers and services.
Without explicit @Inject() decorators, tsx/esbuild cannot resolve
constructor parameters by type, causing undefined service injections
and 500 errors on all API endpoints.

Also fixes the Vite dev proxy to rewrite /api prefix before forwarding
to the control plane, which serves routes without the /api prefix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hub): K8s user impersonation & audit log viewer (#101, #99)

- Add create_client_for_context_as_user() with Impersonate-User/Group
  headers via kube-rs auth_info (engine/client.rs)
- Connect route now uses impersonated client based on JWT identity
- Add user_can_access_cluster() access-control placeholder (auth.rs)
- Add GET /api/v1/audit?limit=N endpoint for viewing audit log entries
- Pod logs endpoint now audits the authenticated user actor
- Add AuditQuery request type and register /audit route

Closes #101, Closes #99

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add explicit @Inject() to EventsModule services for tsx compatibility

tsx (esbuild) does not support emitDecoratorMetadata, so NestJS cannot
resolve constructor parameters by type alone. Services using explicit
@Inject(TOKEN) worked, but EventsModule services relying on implicit
type-based resolution for ModuleRef received undefined, crashing
QueueWorkerEventsService.onModuleInit on startup.

Add @Inject() to all constructor parameters in:
- QueueWorkerEventsService (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- DomainEventBroadcasterAdapter (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- EventBroadcasterService (EventsGateway)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hub): add OIDC authentication scaffolding (#100)

- Add auth module with OidcConfig, AuthUser, JWT claim decoding,
  auth middleware, and auth routes (login, callback, logout, me)
- When OIDC_ENABLED is unset (default), all requests pass through
  as anonymous; when enabled, Bearer JWT is required on API routes
- Wire auth middleware on /api/v1/* routes via Router::nest
- Add /auth/login, /auth/callback (501 placeholders), /auth/logout,
  /auth/me endpoints outside the auth-protected API nest
- Add actor field to AuditEntry for user attribution in audit logs
- Update connect/disconnect handlers to log authenticated user email
- Add base64 dependency for JWT payload decoding

Full Azure Entra ID integration (discovery, token validation, PKCE)
requires client registration and is out of scope for this scaffold.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): wire API layer to hub HTTP endpoints (#98)

Replace webFallback() stub returns with real fetch calls to the hub
service at /api/v1/*. All read operations (contexts, pods, resources,
events, logs, helm, metrics, CRDs, search, etc.) now hit the hub.
Write operations (scale, delete, apply, exec, port-forward, helm
rollback) are deferred to a future iteration.

Hub URL defaults to http://localhost:3001 and can be overridden via
window.__TELESCOPE_HUB_URL__.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add telescope-hub axum HTTP server (#102)

Headless HTTP/WebSocket server exposing Telescope engine capabilities
to web clients via REST API, replacing Tauri IPC.

Implements 15 endpoints:
- GET /healthz — health check
- GET /api/v1/contexts — list kubeconfig contexts
- POST /api/v1/connect — connect to a context
- POST /api/v1/disconnect — disconnect
- GET /api/v1/connection-state — current connection state
- GET /api/v1/resources — list resources by GVK
- GET /api/v1/pods — list pods
- GET /api/v1/events — list events
- GET /api/v1/namespaces — list namespaces
- GET /api/v1/pods/{ns}/{name}/logs — pod logs
- GET /api/v1/cluster-info — cluster version/auth info
- GET /api/v1/search — search cached resources
- GET /api/v1/helm/releases — list Helm releases
- GET /api/v1/metrics/pods — pod metrics
- GET /api/v1/crds — list CRDs
- GET /ws — WebSocket endpoint (echo/placeholder)

Includes Dockerfile for containerized deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(m65b): desktop finalization — expand watchers, container details, action gaps, tests

Expand Resource Watchers (was 3, now 13):
- Added real-time watching for Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets,
  Services, ConfigMaps, Jobs, CronJobs, Ingresses, PVCs, ReplicaSets
- All resources now sync via live watch streams (not just on-demand)

Container Details:
- PodTable: Image column, enhanced status detection (CrashLoopBackOff,
  ImagePullBackOff, OOMKilled, init container states)
- Pod detail: resource requests/limits per container, owner references

Action Gaps Fixed:
- Rollout restart: now supports StatefulSets + DaemonSets (was Deployment-only)
- Rollout status: supports StatefulSets
- Delete: added Ingress, PVC, ReplicaSet support (was 9 types, now 12)

Integration Tests:
- Cluster-agnostic assertions (works on AKS and k3d)
- Added: can_list_deployments, can_list_services, metrics_api_check
- All 8 tests pass against live AKS cluster

99 Rust + 36 web = 135 tests pass.

repository.name: Telescope
repository.description: Telescope: memory-efficient Kubernetes IDE (AKS-first) with desktop (Tauri) + web client

commit.message:
fix: audit unwrap() calls and add ErrorMessage component

Task 1 — Rust unwrap() audit:
- watcher.rs: Replace 8 Mutex::lock().unwrap() calls with lock_store()
  helper that recovers from mutex poisoning via into_inner()
- helm.rs: Replace as_object().unwrap() with is_some_and() pattern;
  add // safe: comment on literal regex unwrap
- main.rs: Add // safe: comment on parent().unwrap() (always has parent)

Task 2 — Error state UX:
- Create ErrorMessage.svelte with icon, suggestion, and retry button
- Create error-suggestions.ts mapping common K8s errors to user-friendly
  suggestions (connection refused, 401/403, timeout, kubelogin, etc.)
- Wire ErrorMessage into pods, helm, and overview pages replacing raw
  error text
- Add unit tests for getSuggestion helper (8 tests)
- Clean up unused error CSS from replaced inline error blocks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Telescope
repository.description: Telescope: memory-efficient Kubernetes IDE (AKS-first) with desktop (Tauri) + web client

commit.message:
fix: harden port-forward limits, exec output cap, and schema migrations

Port forward (S7 — #103):
- Add AtomicUsize counter, reject if >10 concurrent forwards
- Validate remote_port in range 1-65535
- Wrap accept loop with 1-hour idle timeout
- Decrement counter when forward task ends

Exec output (S8 — #104):
- Replace read_to_end with bounded_read (max 10MB per stream)
- Wrap entire exec in 5-minute timeout
- Return clear error messages for both limits

Schema migration (#105):
- Add schema_version table in init()
- Add get/set_schema_version helpers
- Add migrate() runner called on open() after init()
- Version 1 = current schema; future migrations slot in sequentially

Closes #103, closes #104, closes #105

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
